name: Coxe, Curtis Carlisle
description: Curtis is a fine example of the brilliant athlete and the unusual student. He has always taken part in every branch of athletics with marked success but has never allowed his participation to interfere with his studies.

name: Johnson, Clifton Drew
description: An ambitious young man, he is doing graduate work already; independent, he is depending on his own resources to educate himself.

name: Matlack, Marion Brooks
high school: Florida Military Academy and Rollins College
clubs: American Association for Advancement of Science
description: If love of one's calling will serve to advance one, then without doubt Matlack will be a leader in the field of chemistry, for his home address is the Chemistry Laboratory, University of Florida.

name: Raa, Bertel
description: "Miss Raa" is not only a perfect lady, but is one of the most accomplished musicians in the University. He hopes to be a virtuoso and if virtue will help in the accomplishment of this ambition, his virtuosity will be unexcelled.

name: Thomas, O. Otto
description: The holder of the University record and the embossed leather medal awarded to the most prolific distributor of b—perhaps we might say conversation. An entertaining talker who is at his best if you let him tell you what he knows about medicine, surgery and aviation.

name: Harry, M. Merchant
description: Harry combines the qualities of industry and social leadership since he is not only a good student but, being a citizen of Gainesville, has the advantage of the rest of us in that he is a "brother" to all of the ladies.

name: Stein, Maurice
description: "Mawruss," the Trotsky of the campus, is of the "intelligentsia." Ready to inform anybody on any subject, he will hook his nose under the other fellow's and proceed to inform him, yea, even if it be an angel from heaven, he would tell him how to burnish his wings, or tune his harp, or escort tired souls over the dark abyss.

name: Weedon, Frederick R.
description: Fred, the philosopher and poet, whose mind in search of pabulum, plumbs the depths of Tartarus or scales the heights of Olympus, and like a plant with mighty tendrils, stretches out and draws into it what it wishes. He is a disciple of Schopenhauer, the gloomy one, but may he yet adopt a more pleasant master.

name: Baker, Paul O'Brien
description: We all hope that "Bake" never graduates, for the old place will never be the same after he leaves. He started in the dim past but the war came along and he was lost to us for two years. The best tackle that ever graced our gridiron.

name: Anderson, Charles Parke
description: Parke has grown a fierce mustachio for the purpose of preventing persons from confusing him with "Crip" of the same surname. They were so much alike, so handsome, so strong, and yet so gentle. Now they can tell by the tickle.

name: Bishop, Albert Kent
description: This is the great unknown, the mystery man, the sphinx of the Ag. College. He prides himself that he knows fewer students than any man who ever attended the University and that no one, absolutely no one, knows him. It is great to be distinguished in at least one thing.

name: Driffers, Roy Ludford
major: Bachelor of Science in Agricultural Education

name: Bratley, Homer E.
major: Bachelor of Science in Agricultural Education
description: If "'Omer smote 'is bloomin' lyre" he could tell many a romantic tale of the ancient cities of old Europe, for he is a much-travelled man. Since he is to be a teacher it is hardly fair to tell of his many adventures in arms and in love, for he is yet unmarried and he must needs be reputed as a quiet and unadventurous scholar.

name: Gunn, June Rawls
major: Bachelor of Science in Agriculture, and Bachelor of Science in Education
description: June is one of our oldtimers who left to answer the call to arms and was thus thrown behind. A loss in time but not in the esteem in which he has always been held. He is one of the most modest, unassuming, energetic of men.

name: DeForest, Lewis Christiance
description: We'll stack up this he-vamp of ours against the claimants for similar honors in any other university or college in the country. He's best at that, but is also a star theatrical performer in either male or female characters and equally good in the role of a "hard-boiled" officer.

name: Mahoney, William Henry
clubs: President of the Senior Class of the College of Agriculture
First Lieutenant in R. O. T. C. (4)
description: He is a star at writing up farm dope. A nice fellow who should turn his talents toward selling real estate or something more genteel than tilling the soil. But then, there's no need to worry, for if he farms he'll be one of the few who do after an ag. course.

name: Goldsby, Jack Kaufman
description: Jack, the good-natured, was for four years the pride of our football team, one of the mainstays of the line, and an extraordinary player. He was one of the first to fight, being one of the first to volunteer for the army in which he spent more than two years.

name: Tan, Len Bo
description: Our little Oriental has endeared himself to all with whom he has come in contact for he has all of the qualities that go to make a friend. We expect to hear of him as a professor in one of the colleges of his native land in a short time for that is his ambition.

name: Heuck, Carl Peter
major: Bachelor of Science in Agriculture, Iowa State College
description: Heuck is not a "square-head" notwithstanding his name which (for the benefit of the uninformed) is pronounced as one would cough, with the vowel sound of "oil." During the time since he migrated from Iowa he has made many friends who hope the "orange orchard" will be a success.

name: Hamilton, Henry Glenn
major: Bachelor of Science in Agricultural Education
clubs: Ag. College Football and Basketball Teams (4)
description: "Hill Billy" came from the wilds of the moonshiners' hills of old Tennessee. After a long process he has become fully civilized and a lowlander can associate with him without fear of any sort. Old "Billy" may not always be right but if he goes wrong it will be a mistake of the head and not of the heart.

name: Adams, Alto L.
clubs: Winner, Declamation Contest for Trustee Medal (1)
description: Alto Soprano is the most serious man now gracing our campus. If he smiles it is only an accident. He has no time for the petty frivolities of life but is a lover of Blackstone and his disciples. Although often sought, he has never cared for the social life of the University and has deprived of an intimacy with him that would have been profitable all but those who could teach him.

name: Clark, Joseph Stanton
high school: Augusta Military Academy and St. Edwards College
description: Joe is a natural leader and had he been with us longer his talents would have forced recognition of the fact. Even though he has attended here one year only, yet hardly a man but knows him, for Joe is always starting something that makes a "hit."

name: Watkins, Joseph Nielsen
major: Bachelor of Science in Civil Engineering
description: Joe Watkins is a "conch" from Key West but has been in the United States long enough to be civilized. A business-like young man who has little time for foolishness and with it all a good fellow to "chum" with.

name: Boring, Elmer Wesley
description: Elmer is a shrinking violet in ordinary contact with his fellow students but in capacity for work he has the characteristics of a horse. It will be a pleasure to see him graduate for he has overcome the obstacle of ill health and has held on by sheer grit and determination when many another would have stopped.

name: Hardee, Charles Jay
description: C. J. is first of all a politician, second a baseball player, and next a student of law, but he does them all with unvarying success. If he lets his abilities loose in the direction of politics after graduation, nothing will be out of his reach.

name: Edrehi, Montrose
description: The little man with the big voice, and incidentally, with no mean calibre mind. He will be turned loose on the people as a lawyer.

name: Liddon, John Walker
description: The brilliant son of an illustrious father. But, oh, so lazy. Mentally without a superior but physically a sluggard, fortunately he has chosen the field of law in which to exercise his talents. Just to think he might be what is so much better, a big league pitcher, if he only would.

name: Getzen, Samuel Wyche
description: Colonel Getzen slipped one over on the law faculty; he went up to the Supreme Court, took the exam, and was admitted to the bar. Thus has he overridden all obstacles when it seemed as if everything was conspiring against him to prevent him from achieving his life ambition, to be able to call himself a lawyer.

name: Norton, Oscar Harris
description: Politician extraordinary, football player, and excellent student, combines many talents with great ability. If he follows the law, he will be pre-eminent in that field; if he enters politics, he will be "boss" of his county.

name: Mellor, Frederick Harvey
description: For Fred's sake it is to be hoped that the University does not become co-ed while he is here for the lonesome few that we have had have almost wrecked him. How unfortunate to be so appealing to the ladies, and with it all, so unwittingly charming.

name: Quinan, Edward Botsford
description: Eddie is getting an early start in the legal profession but he came to the Law College with collegiate training and considerable experience of a diversified nature. With his bright mind and steady application he has a brilliant future.

name: Madison, William McKee
description: Bill Madison is the best example of general ability in every field that can be cited among the members of his class. An extraordinary student, a fair athlete, and a general good fellow, liked by all, the "Admirable Crichton" of the Class of 1921.

name: Scruggs, Sigsbee Lee
description: Sigsbee has been with us for some time, but was one of the unfortunates whose college work was wrecked by the war. He will graduate next year with the degree of Bachelor of Laws. A wizard, for although married, he can attend college.

name: Peacock, Samuel Clarence
description: Sam's name is the synonym for work, for he has more irons in the fire than any man here in recent years. If he were not so busy with his various projects Sam could find more time to be the good fellow that he can. It is a matter of regret that more cannot be among the ones who are his friends.

name: Vining, E. Clyde
description: If it were a crime to work, Clyde would spend the remainder of his days in durance vile. But if labor reaps success, he will be more amply rewarded than any of our acquaintances. One must love his chosen work to live with it as Clyde does.

name: Savage, Charles Augustus, Jr.
clubs: Law College Football and Baseball Teams (4)
description: Charley the soldier, or Charley the lawyer, it is difficult to decide which is his true love, for he is a star in both fields and impartial it seems to each. Whether he chooses a military or legal career, needless to say Charley will seek that "golden mediocrity which merits neither envy nor ridicule."

name: Hamilton, George Creary
description: If this maxim is true, Creary will sooner or later have a corner on knowledge, for he leaves the University equipped as a teacher and, at the same time, one of the best students ever to graduate here, with an exhaustible fund of information.

name: Cason, Shafter Woodford
description: The man who will put Otter Creek on the map even as one other famous man has distinguished Gum Center. To reach home Cason goes by rail to Williston, which is some twenty miles from Gainesville, walks twelve miles to the big creek which he must swim, and then his Uncle meets him with a buggy and drives thirteen miles to home.

name: Franklin, James Alfred
description: Jim is one of whom we can say little. All we can do is look and gasp in awe that one of such comparative youth should in the short space of twenty-odd years have become so all-knowing, all-seeing, and all-wise. Great will be his future and proud will be he who can say that he has known, seen, or touched hands with James A. Franklin.

name: Friedlander, Herbert Milton
description: Friedlander is a live one, active in everything except the "Maccabean." He is particularly active in his efforts to down the "hated lawyers" in whatever they try to manage, thus showing his patriotism as well as high order of intelligence. When he grows older and more matured we expect to see Herbert shine as a promoter.

name: Williams, Leslie Dodd
description: How one can be so young and yet so wise is mysterious. Leslie is one of the best students in his class, even one of the best in the University. Yet he has time and leisure for any student activity that interests him. It is given to him to be popular and yet to be intellectual.

name: Nichols, Cecil H.
major: Bachelor of Science in Agricultural Education
description: He is one of the serious individuals of the campus. He refuses to be a boy any longer for he has put away childish things and clings to the things that count. A bright student who means to make good in his future undertakings.

name: Wells, William G.
description: No student ever had more unsought honors thrust upon him than "Willie Green." Not a politician in any sense of the word, his fellow students, recognizing his worth, have seen fit to honor him with the most important offices in their power to bestow. A living example of the efficacy of industry and modesty.

name: Almond, John Dixie Jr.
major: Bachelor of Science in Electrical Engineering
description: Johnny is a fish, not in the ordinary sense, but in the water. He is an expert swimmer whose natural element seems to be water rather than land. Likable and modest but over retiring, he underestimates his ability, which is great.

name: Pfeiffer, Charles Alfred
major: Bachelor of Science in Civil Engineering, Clemson College
description: Charley, our likable cheer leader, is the personification of "pep," the livest fellow ever seen. With all his interest in many student activities, he still has time to be a good soldier and better than average student.

name: Gunn, William Walter
major: Bachelor of Science in Electrical Engineering
description: Young and yet full of honors, Walter leaves the University with the friendship of all with whom he came in contact; a tireless worker who does all things well.

name: Hartman, George William
major: Bachelor of Science in Civil Engineering
description: George, the dependable, has been for the past several years our mainstay in the pitcher's box. So capable has he been that the managership of the team has been forced upon him for three years.

name: Knight, Daniel B.
major: Bachelor of Science in Electrical Engineering
clubs: designated Honor Graduate to receive regular Army Commission
description: "Bloxy" is a star in military circles; the mainstay of the military establishment for he can fill any position from that of "buck" number four in the rear rank to Battalion Commander. How one can be such a good soldier and yet so likable is a mystery.

name: McCoy, Hubbard
major: Bachelor of Science in Electrical Engineering
description: Coy is one of the best loved and most highly respected of the entire student body of the University. A quiet, industrious, studious chap who does his best in all that he undertakes.

name: McKey, Wallace Alexander
major: Bachelor of Science in Civil Engineering
description: Always is one of the leaders in progressive thought and is one of the best liked of students. It can always be said of him that, whatever he has done, he has done his best and has followed the course that conscience dictates.

name: Huff, Van Ellis
major: Bachelor of Science in Civil Engineering
description: How can a man be the leader of society and yet be classified as a "roughneck" engineer? Van is both of them but with only a slight accent on the "roughneck" part and a strong emphasis on the "society leader." Still Van is also one of the best students in the College of Engineering for it's easy to get in with the Engineers but hard to get out, that is with a degree.

name: Moorhead, J. Robert, Jr.
major: Bachelor of Science in Civil Engineering
description: "Jarhead" has little time for the childish things that those of more youthful spirits indulge in. How well he has made it a part of him, is a matter of common knowledge. Ask the profs.; they know.

name: Ford, Herbert Giddens
major: Bachelor of Science in Chemical Engineering
description: A returned soldier who gave two years of his college career to the army and is consequently so much behind his original class. He has been honored in being selected as the representative of the state of Florida at Oxford University as a Rhodes Scholar. So generally has he been esteemed that he has reaped the highest honors in college.

name: Thomas, Clarence Strouse
major: Bachelor of Science in Electrical Engineering
description: The laziest man. His only exercise, except during football season, is breathing. He eats pre-digested food only. His aversion to work is physical only, for he is a very alert student.

name: Tatom, Louis Jeter
major: Bachelor of Science in Electrical Engineering
clubs: honor graduate to receive regular Army Commission
description: A self-made man, but oh! how he loves his maker. There is no danger of Louis under-rating himself, but then, he has such great ability that he can hardly mark himself up too high. A most conscientious worker who is destined to accomplish much in his chosen field.

name: De Sampaio, Jose
major: Bachelor of Science in Civil Engineering
description: Joe is an example of grit and determination in that he determined to come to the United States, acquire an education, and fit himself for service to his country. He has overcome the great handicap of speaking a foreign tongue and now equals the native North American student in every way. And further, Joe is the best of good fellows and very popular with all, students and ladies.

name: Newell, B. Davis
description: "Snaky" Davis, the far-famed snake-charmer, better known as "Makaka," was one of the hits of the Carnival. He eats 'em alive, or any other way. But this is not his only accomplishment for he is one of the stars in Dr. Crow's French classes, being so proficient and loving his work so well that he has remained an extra year to pursue his studies in Modern Languages further.

name: Mckey, John Dwight
major: Bachelor of Science in Electrical Engineering
description: He came from N. C. A. & M., in good standing too, but that shouldn't be a matter of surprise as to Johnny, the veteran of the Mexican border, where he learned the gentle art from the "Mecks." Also a long-time member of the A. E. F., where his sweet tenor, wine-soaked, made such a hit with the "Y" and S. A. lassies.
